steps.title: Find zeros in the table
steps.content:
A factor comes from an $x$-value where the polynomial equals $0$. The table shows
$$p(-1)=0$$
and
$$p(2)=0$$

steps.title: Translate zeros to factors
steps.content: By the Factor Theorem, if $p(r)=0$, then $x-r$ is a factor. So $p(-1)=0$ gives $x+1$, and $p(2)=0$ gives $x-2$.

steps.title: Match both factors
steps.content:
The product using both factors is
$$(x+1)(x-2)$$
The answer is D.
